支持吓先

係咪香港有LLB但冇牌都可以過去考conversion?
你指做律師的話，無conversion比你考，但係可以比Board去assess你個LLB需要補幾多科先可以符合基本資格然後過黎讀個Practical Legal Training半年先可以注冊做律師同攞到職業評估。

但咁樣拎PR係咪都好難？
Yes.

定建議讀第科master?例如幼教？
你想讀咩先。

有冇澳洲會計師熟澳洲tax 啲嘢
我預計自己三年內會做到技術移民
假設係香港有兩層樓，
一層做左按揭買 ，諗住幾十年都唔會賣，比父母長住。
另一層就已經供完，都係諗住香港放租投資用，必要時轉名。 暫時唔諗 賣物業呢個option

關於第一個做緊按揭嘅物業，諗到兩個option
1.轉名：做到tax resident前轉名比父母，只牽涉本地印花稅，最開心，但父母有聯名嘅property, 要甩左名先得。另外佢地有幫細佬做擔保，驚銀行唔批新按揭而轉唔到名。

2.唔轉名：擺住係度，父母長住，佢地遲啲唔住就租出去，租出去收番嘅錢就係澳洲呢邊照比income tax, 幾十年後再諗賣唔賣出去，因為capital gain tax好重稅；或者到時轉名比屋企人。

所以
問題1: 想了解下萬一我做左tax resident後先轉名個stamp duty ，澳洲點計有關海外物業轉名嘅稅項？

問題2: 如果我唔賣香港個物業，香港銀行做緊個按揭可唔可以用黎係澳洲扣到稅？

問題3: 移民係唔係一定要物業甩身？ 甩唔到身又點處理？

quote.msg: 聽日財政預算案，唔知會唔會有機會公布pr pathway詳情？

replies.msg:
無PR pathway.

岩岩公佈既澳洲聯邦預算案，關於移民方面既概括：
1. 2020-2021 移民配額為 160,000 (注: 配額 不等於 最終移民人數)
2. 家庭類移民配額從 上年既47,732 增加至 今年既 77,300 (注: 應該係為左解決配偶移民既積聚case backlog)
3. 雇主擔保, GTI, 商業投資移民申請將會撥入技術類並被優先處理
4. 境内申請 和 擔保係偏遠地區的配偶申請 將會被優先處理
5. GTI 配額將會從 上年既5,000 增加至 今年既 15,000
6. 商業投資移民將會從 上年既6,800 增加至 今年既 13,500

總結: 總配額不變既情況下，技術移民 (189/190/491) 應該會減少配額去分配比GTI同商業投資移民。GTI同投資移民將會係今年既大勢，建議有能力人士以呢兩個渠道優先考慮申請。

邊境完全開放有可能要等到明年下半年，大家要過黎讀書的話要考慮好時間了。

由巴，想問吓知唔知要咩條件先得？

「老公之前係有澳洲永久居民(Australian Permanent Resident，簡稱P.R.) 簽證，但因為當時要住滿2年先可以申請入藉。他後來在移民局網站找到一條特別條例，就係透過澳洲移民部部長的酌情權(Ministerial discretion) 申請入籍成為澳洲公民。於是試一試去申請，好幸運地獲得審批。即是在香港而毋須在澳洲坐移民監或居留就可以宣誓入藉澳洲。」

Kimmy 都再三提醒成功申請的例子其實十分罕有，不過總算是多一個途徑，而過程中只需要交齊文件和通過入藉考試，不用面試，成功後只須在香港澳洲領事館宣誓入籍便完成程序。

「老公之前係有澳洲永久居民(Australian Permanent Resident，簡稱P.R.) 簽證，但因為當時要住滿2年先可以申請入藉。他後來在移民局網站找到一條特別條例，就係透過澳洲移民部部長的酌情權(Ministerial discretion) 申請入籍成為澳洲公民。於是試一試去申請，好幸運地獲得審批。即是在香港而毋須在澳洲坐移民監或居留就可以宣誓入藉澳洲。」

Kimmy 都再三提醒成功申請的例子其實十分罕有，不過總算是多一個途徑，而過程中只需要交齊文件和通過入藉考試，不用面試，成功後只須在香港澳洲領事館宣誓入籍便完成程序。

replies.msg:
https://immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/form-listing/forms/1290.pdf

Ministerial discretions

1. If you were in Australia as an unlawful non-citizen during the 4 years immediately before making your application and it was as the result of an administrative error, then that period of time may be treated as a period of lawful residence in Australia.
2. If you were in Australia as a lawful non-citizen but not as a permanent resident as the result of an administrative error, then that period of time may be treated as a period of permanent residence.
3. If you were in Australia as a lawful non-citizen, but not as a permanent resident during the 12 months immediately before application, then that period of time may be treated as a period of permanent residence if you would otherwise suffer significant hardship or disadvantage.
4. Time spent in prison or a psychiatric institution by order of a court in the 4 years immediately before making your application may be counted as time in Australia if your conviction has been quashed.
5. If you are the spouse or de facto partner of an Australian citizen, or surviving spouse or de facto partner of an Australian citizen at the time you make your application, and you spent time outside Australia as a permanent resident in the 4 years immediately before making your
application, you may treat those periods of absence as timein Australia if, during each period of absence:
• you were the spouse or de facto partner of that Australian citizen, and
• they were an Australian citizen, and
• you maintained a close and continuing association with Australia.
6. If you spent time outside Australia as a permanent resident in the 12 months immediately before making your application, as the holder of an interdependent visa and your interdependent partner was an Australian citizen during your periods of absence, you may treat those periods of absence as time in Australia if you had a close and continuing association with Australia during those periods of absence.

replies.msg:
9004 Subclass 485 visas granted before 9 July 2020

(1) This clause applies to a Subclass 485 (Temporary Graduate) visa if:

(a) the visa was granted before 9 July 2020; and

(b) the visa was granted on the basis that the holder satisfied the primary criteria for the grant of the visa; and

(c) on the date of grant of the visa, the holder held a Hong Kong passport; and

(d) the visa did not cease to be in effect before 9 July 2020.

(2) This clause also applies to a Subclass 485 (Temporary Graduate) visa (the secondary visa) if:

(a) the secondary visa was granted before 9 July 2020; and

(b) the secondary visa was granted on the basis that the holder satisfied the secondary criteria for the grant of the visa as a member of the family unit of a person (the primary applicant) who satisfied the primary criteria for the grant of a Subclass 485 (Temporary Graduate) visa (the primary visa); and

(c) on the date of grant of the primary visa, the primary applicant held a Hong Kong passport; and

(d) the secondary visa did not cease to be in effect before 9 July 2020.

(3) Despite clause 485.511 of Schedule 2 to these Regulations, a visa to which this clause applies is a temporary visa permitting the holder to:

(a) travel to, and enter, Australia on multiple occasions; and

(b) remain in Australia;

during the period that:

(c) started when the visa came into effect; and

(d) ends on 8 July 2025.

(d) the visa did not cease to be in effect before 9 July 2020.

(2) This clause also applies to a Subclass 485 (Temporary Graduate) visa (the secondary visa) if:

(a) the secondary visa was granted before 9 July 2020; and

(b) the secondary visa was granted on the basis that the holder satisfied the secondary criteria for the grant of the visa as a member of the family unit of a person (the primary applicant) who satisfied the primary criteria for the grant of a Subclass 485 (Temporary Graduate) visa (the primary visa); and

(c) on the date of grant of the primary visa, the primary applicant held a Hong Kong passport; and

(d) the secondary visa did not cease to be in effect before 9 July 2020.

(3) Despite clause 485.511 of Schedule 2 to these Regulations, a visa to which this clause applies is a temporary visa permitting the holder to:

(a) travel to, and enter, Australia on multiple occasions; and

(b) remain in Australia;

during the period that:

(c) started when the visa came into effect; and

(d) ends on 8 July 2025.

Ministerial discretions

1. If you were in Australia as an unlawful non-citizen during the 4 years immediately before making your application and it was as the result of an administrative error, then that period of time may be treated as a period of lawful residence in Australia.
2. If you were in Australia as a lawful non-citizen but not as a permanent resident as the result of an administrative error, then that period of time may be treated as a period of permanent residence.
3. If you were in Australia as a lawful non-citizen, but not as a permanent resident during the 12 months immediately before application, then that period of time may be treated as a period of permanent residence if you would otherwise suffer significant hardship or disadvantage.
4. Time spent in prison or a psychiatric institution by order of a court in the 4 years immediately before making your application may be counted as time in Australia if your conviction has been quashed.
5. If you are the spouse or de facto partner of an Australian citizen, or surviving spouse or de facto partner of an Australian citizen at the time you make your application, and you spent time outside Australia as a permanent resident in the 4 years immediately before making your
application, you may treat those periods of absence as timein Australia if, during each period of absence:
• you were the spouse or de facto partner of that Australian citizen, and
• they were an Australian citizen, and
• you maintained a close and continuing association with Australia.
6. If you spent time outside Australia as a permanent resident in the 12 months immediately before making your application, as the holder of an interdependent visa and your interdependent partner was an Australian citizen during your periods of absence, you may treat those periods of absence as time in Australia if you had a close and continuing association with Australia during those periods of absence.

唔係話必須要持學生簽證係境外先可以計ASR。
如果我之前已拎visa咁無問題，online讀係照計，不過問題係我地係未拎到visa，係佢未批既情況下計唔計，澳洲好似都無針對呢個情況解釋清楚？

我岩岩再諗多一陣，覺得其實無學生簽證既境外 remote learning 都可以計Australia Study Requirement唔太合理，所以專登番去摷番幾個 Legislative Instructments睇番但係都搵唔到。

不過上面個 cap 圖有一句 online study undertaken outside Australia as a result of COVID-19 travel restrictions will count towards the Australian Study Requirement for existing and new student visa holders，即係 online study undertaken outside Australia 有可能只能 for existing and new student visa holders，咁無 student visa 即係無得計。

但係一般移民局 website 信一半就夠，所以我下禮拜再搵機會問下。

呢個只係澳洲legal system既legal procedure。個repeal date並唔代表其他公佈。

如果呢個legislative instructment既purpose係修改法例而唔係補充要求的話，係呢個instructment registered左一段時間之後會被 repealed，但係repealed既只係instructment本身而effect of amendment係會remain the same。

有興趣繼續鑽研的話可以參考 Division 1 of Part 3 of Chapter 3 of the Legislation Act 2003，咩係Automatic repeal。

真係要扯上關係的話，就應該要睇Biden之後既對華政策實際如何，會唔會同Trump一樣強硬。

雖然我個人的確唔喜歡Trump既風格，但係無可否認佢係一個rule-breaker，係對抗中共咁bullying既attitude最有效既方法 (之一)。但係我會question Biden上台後點樣處理中美關係。

畢竟澳洲係睇美國頭，但係又唔係盲目跟隨，呢個5年PR政策又唔係100%淨係睇美帝指示。老實講如果真係為左美國太過得罪中共，澳洲本土既商家可能又會唔開心。所以中間個平衡唔知道ScoMo會點掌握。

結論就係：我地呢D蟻民左右唔黎既就唔好想太多，focus番自己可以控制既事上面仲好。
